meaning: adj.独立存在的,真实的,有实质的,大量的,巨额的n.[语]实词,名词,独立存在的实体
examples: They plan to meet again in Rome very soon to begin substantive negotiations.
Although recent censure of corporate boards of directors as "passive" and "supine" may be excessive, those who criticize board performance have plenty of substantive ammunition.
The report concluded that no substantive changes were necessary.
Does he think that this vote is a matter of substantive policy, or is this gamesmanship?
The substantive, more serious problem, is that revenue target of 19.1 percent of GDP.
